title: MAP 6.13: Specify protocol and semantic validators with halt authority
description: Wave 4 from MAP 6.13 plan. Split hcom/MATOCP protocol compliance from semantic output-correctness validation, specify Layer 1 deterministic checks vs Layer 2 fuzzy judge, define telemetry and halt state. Read MAP_System/artifacts/planning/map-613-master-implementation-plan.md, MAP_System/scripts/validate_events.py, Guidelines/llm-communication-rules.md, Guidelines/6.13/files7/MAP-Validator-Specification.md, and MAP_System/SELF_REPAIR_SYSTEM.md before editing.
acceptance_criteria: Protocol validator spec covers hcom intent, MATOCP token form where applicable, request-format requirements, and false-positive adjudication.
Semantic validator spec states Layer 1 coverage improves catch rate but does not reduce Layer 2 false positives toward 1%; judge accuracy and calibration are separate requirements.
Halt-state spec defines blocking storage, runner/agent-loop response, root-cause/repair requirement, and clear authority.
Probe artifact defines how to demonstrate that a validator can halt dispatch, not only log.
